message:
test(fcp-conformance/raptorq-envelope): pin ZoneKeyIdMismatch fast-fail + AAD/nonce binding (br-xson4)

fcp-raptorq's higher-level SymbolEnvelope wraps the AEAD primitive in
a self-describing struct and exposes a structured ZoneKeyIdMismatch
fast-fail returned BEFORE the AEAD primitive runs when the
caller-supplied zone_key_id does not match the envelope's own. This
fast-fail lets callers distinguish a routing/rotation mistake from a
tampered ciphertext, and is distinct from the lower-level fcp-protocol
envelope already pinned in br-2qkn0.

Adds 13 tests against the public fcp_raptorq::SymbolEnvelope API:

- chacha_round_trip_recovers_plaintext
- xchacha_round_trip_recovers_plaintext
- zone_key_id_mismatch_fast_fails_before_aead (pins the structured
  error AND the (expected, found) payload)
- wrong_zone_key_decrypt_fails_via_aead (key mismatch surfaces as
  DecryptFailed, NOT ZoneKeyIdMismatch — the security signal)
- tampered_ciphertext_decrypt_fails
- flipped_auth_tag_byte_decrypt_fails
- tampered_object_id_in_aad_decrypt_fails
- tampered_epoch_id_in_aad_decrypt_fails (cross-epoch replay defense)
- tampered_source_id_decrypt_fails (subkey binding)
- tampered_sender_instance_id_decrypt_fails (subkey + XChaCha nonce
  binding)
- tampered_frame_seq_decrypt_fails_via_nonce
- tampered_esi_decrypt_fails_via_nonce
- chacha_envelope_does_not_decrypt_under_xchacha_selector (algorithm
  cross-rejection)

All 13 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
raptorq_envelope_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
